compiled_state.title: Tier E E3: port write/lifecycle cmds (transition/reopen/claim/compact/compact-all/scratch/delete) in-process + E5c ticket_txn relocation
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Why
E3 is the write/lifecycle cluster of Tier E (story adult-oxide-slave / kickoff lad-cipher-arena):
port the ~7 still-bash commands the dispatcher routes to (transition, reopen, claim, compact,
compact-all, scratch, delete) in-process over rebar._commands/_store/graph/reducer. Coupled to
E5c: relocate the ticket_txn.py locked critical section into the rebar package so transition/claim
run in-process without putting the engine dir on sys.path (test_engine_dir guard). Touches the
single locked write path + the optimistic-concurrency exit-10 contract. Plan opus-reviewed
(REVISE -> 11 revisions folded in). No bash deleted (back-loads to E7/E8). No kill-switch; rollback = git revert.

## What
Per-command pattern (E0-E2): characterize bash arm -> port reusing rebar.* (one source for error
strings) -> route argparse in-process + rewire library off _run -> dual-run byte-parity test
(tests/interfaces/test_e3_*.py) -> green commit + child ticket. Sub-commits:
- E5c: rebar/_commands/txn.py {transition_core, claim_core}; reuse ONLY event_append.event_filename
  + hand-rolled rename/add/commit under ONE lock-hold (NOT stage_and_commit: re-locks per event +
  sorted bytes). Preserve json.dump(ensure_ascii=False). Convert sys.exit/stderr -> return + typed
  exceptions; old _engine/ticket_txn.py becomes a thin shim re-exiting 10/1/2. Preserve dual-leg
  fcntl+mkdir lock (stiff-mop-lane fix). Golden-bytes test (STATUS + CLAIM, before/after identical).
- Port ticket-unblock.py batch-close/newly_unblocked -> rebar/graph/_unblock.py (faithful: exclude
  already-unblocked, tombstone-aware, sorted(listdir) ordering pins UNBLOCKED byte-order); shim the .py.
- transition: full 486-line wrapper split into (a) validation/no-op/ghost/init, (b) close-path
  (children guard + unblock + force-close comment + compact-on-close + scratch cleanup), (c) un-archive
  seam archived->open (in-process revert + live-ARCHIVED scan + exact reason; skips --output/UNBLOCKED).
  reopen folds in as transition(closed,open).
- claim: over claim_core; reproduce _emit_error_envelope stderr (ticket_not_found/concurrency_conflict/
  claim_failed), not just happy path CLAIMED:.
- CONCURRENCY MATRIX rerun (-m integration): writer storm, claim storm, two-clone convergence.
- compact/compact-all: TICKET_TRACKER_DIR (singular), 4 skip/success strings, --no-commit/--skip-sync;
  then rewire transition compact-on-close off the subprocess.
- scratch: filesystem-only, NO init; set/get/clear JSON envelopes.
- delete (own ticket, LAST, after matrix green): port ticket-delete-unlink-scan.py faithfully
  (net-active-link order), children guard + --user-approved strings, UNLINK + STATUS(deleted) +
  ARCHIVED + tombstone/.archived in one commit; shares ported unblock logic.
Fold in: git config user.name -> "Unknown" fallback parity; 2-arg autodetect + =/space flag variants
in parity scenarios; close bug stiff-mop-lane via preserved dual-leg lock.

## Scope
The 7 write/lifecycle commands + E5c relocation + the unblock-logic port. NOT in scope: store/locking/
event-schema design changes (ratified sound); E4 init/fsck; E5/E5b bridge+reconciler (Jira gate);
E6-E9. Canonicalization of event bytes (sorted keys) is a different epic (P1.0) -- preserve current
unsorted bytes here.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] rebar._commands.txn houses transition_core/claim_core; _engine/ticket_txn.py is a thin shim re-exiting 10/1/2 identically
- [ ] All 7 commands routed in-process in rebar._cli; library functions rewired off _run subprocess
- [ ] newly_unblocked logic ported to rebar.graph; _engine/ticket-unblock.py shimmed
- [ ] Dual-run byte-parity tests (stdout/stderr/exit, text + --output json) green for every command incl. edge cases
- [ ] Golden-bytes test confirms STATUS/CLAIM committed bytes unchanged by the relocation
- [ ] Concurrency matrix (-m integration) green after the write-path relocation
- [ ] Dual-leg fcntl+mkdir lock preserved; bug stiff-mop-lane closed
- [ ] Full suite green at each cluster boundary; no module over 800 LOC; no <100-LOC shards

comments.body: E5c DONE (commit e6e645ae): relocated transition/claim locked critical section from _engine/ticket_txn.py into importable rebar._commands.txn (transition_core/claim_core). Cores RAISE (ConcurrencyMismatch=10, CommandError=1/2) instead of sys.exit; reuse rebar._store.lock (dual-leg fcntl+mkdir, stiff-mop-lane fix preserved) + event_append.event_filename + rebar.reducer. _engine/ticket_txn.py now a thin bash-leg shim (re-exits 10/1/2, preserves positional argv) until E7. Byte-parity preserved (unsorted json.dump, NOT canonical sorted — P1.0 scope); golden-bytes guard added. flock structural guard repointed to new module. GREEN: 51 unit + 4 concurrency-matrix (integration) + 369 interfaces + 4/4 flock guard. NEXT: in-process transition wrapper cutover (port ticket-transition.sh logic incl. open-children guard + newly_unblocked port from ticket-unblock.py) + reopen.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: transition/reopen IN-PROCESS (pending full-suite green + commit): ported ticket-transition.sh wrapper -> rebar._commands.transition (transition_compute + transition_cli + reopen_cli) over txn.transition_core. Also ported ticket-unblock.py batch-close/newly_unblocked -> rebar.graph._unblock (engine file now a thin CLI shim re-exporting the package fns; literal 'deleted' kept for legacy static guard). Routed transition/reopen in-process in rebar._cli (_LIFECYCLE, full init). Rewired rebar.transition library fn off _run -> transition_compute (reopen delegates). Dual-run parity test test_e3_transition.py: 8 scenarios GREEN first run (not-found text+json, usage, invalid/deleted reject, no-op json quirk, 2-arg autodetect, close UNBLOCKED, dependent-unblock). Fixed test_ticket_unblock single-reduce-call mock target to new module. compact-on-close still subprocesses ticket-compact.sh (rewire when compact ported). claim still on _run (next sub-commit).
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: claim IN-PROCESS (pending full-suite green + commit): added claim_compute + claim_cli to rebar._commands.transition (lifecycle wrappers module) over txn.claim_core. Reproduces _emit_error_envelope (ticket_not_found/concurrency_conflict/claim_failed) on stdout in json mode + the resolver not-found prose + claim_core's 'cannot claim' stderr + CLAIMED text/json. Routed claim in-process in rebar._cli (_LIFECYCLE). Rewired rebar.claim library off _run -> claim_compute. Parity test test_e3_claim.py: 7 scenarios GREEN (usage, not-found text+json, --assignee missing-value, bad --output, claim success text/json +/- assignee, already-claimed exit-10 text+json). Concurrency matrix (-m integration, incl. claim storm exercising rewired rebar.claim): 4 passed. WRITE-PATH TRIO (transition/reopen/claim) touching the exit-10 optimistic-concurrency contract now in-process + validated. REMAINING in E3: compact/compact-all, scratch, delete.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: WRITE-PATH TRIO COMPLETE + committed: E5c relocation (e6e645ae), transition/reopen (41217cc7), claim (bf24646d). All of the locked critical section + exit-10 optimistic-concurrency contract is now in-process. Full non-integration suite 1677 passed; concurrency matrix 4 passed. Dual-leg fcntl+mkdir lock preserved (stiff-mop-lane). REMAINING in E3: compact/compact-all (sync-before-compact, forward-compat unknown-type partition, exit 0/1/2/3/10), scratch (filesystem-only, no init), delete (destructive, --user-approved + children guard + UNLINK scan). Paused for user review at the write-path boundary.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: scratch (46caa2cb) + delete (pending) DONE. delete: ported ticket_delete + ticket-delete-unlink-scan.py -> rebar._commands.delete (scan_and_write_unlinks with fast-path, children guard CREATE-based, STATUS(deleted)+ARCHIVED+tombstone+.archived in one atomic commit, --user-approved guard, idempotent re-invocation silent, newly_unblocked). Routed in _cli (full init); CLI-only (no lib/MCP). Parity test test_e3_delete.py: 6 green (no-approval, arity, not-found text+json, children guard, success text+json, UNLINK-clears-dep, idempotent). 54 delete-related tests green; concurrency matrix 4 green. Full suite pending. E3 COMMANDS ALL PORTED: transition/reopen/claim/compact/compact-all/scratch/delete.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: E3 COMPLETE — all 7 write/lifecycle commands in-process, 6 green commits: E5c relocate txn core (e6e645ae), transition/reopen + unblock port (41217cc7), claim (bf24646d), compact/compact-all + compact-on-close rewire (9f7bd246), scratch (46caa2cb), delete (7ddced5c). AC review: (1) rebar._commands.txn houses transition_core/claim_core, _engine/ticket_txn.py is a re-exiting shim ✓; (2) all 7 routed in rebar._cli, library transition/reopen/claim/compact rewired off _run (scratch/delete CLI-only) ✓; (3) newly_unblocked ported to rebar.graph._unblock, engine ticket-unblock.py shimmed ✓; (4) dual-run byte-parity tests green for every command incl. edge cases (test_e3_transition/claim/compact/scratch/delete) ✓; (5) golden-bytes guard test_e3_txn_bytes ✓; (6) concurrency matrix green post-relocation ✓; (7) dual-leg lock preserved, stiff-mop-lane closed ✓; (8) full suite 1694 passed at boundary, no module >800 LOC (transition.py 593), new modules all >100 LOC (ticket-unblock.py 96 is a transitional strangler shim, deleted in E7) ✓. NOTE for E7/E8: bash NOT deleted — dispatcher arms + ticket-*.sh + ticket_txn.py/ticket-unblock.py shims + ticket-delete-unlink-scan.py remain as the parity 2nd impl until the dispatcher + .sh suites retire together.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
